explanation_box.text: Well, he's green so he blends in with his environment to hide from predators. He looks unison in shape and he has the ability to balance on a branch, meaning he can likely run and survive from predators. He does not look like much of a predator himself, however.
Ah, it's because of his toes. His toes are different from the reptile with the higher fertility score. Maybe sharp toes makes it harder to grab things?
This one is obvious, he's red. He sticks out like a sore thumb, predators will have a field day with him.
Same reason as the last red guy, he sticks out like a sore thumb and can easily be spotted for consumption by a larger predator.

I guess the round toes actually decrease his fertility at the same time as being red? Maybe cause with the sharp toes he could at least fight back to a predator now with the red skin and round toes he stands no chance against a predator.

He's green so he blends in with his environment. He's got the sharp toes though which I'm still not sure helps him or hurts him. I think the sharp toes help him because he can slice a predator if he gets caught.
Red skin and pointy toes. So he attracts a predator and then fights him with his pointy toes I guess.
This is the ideal reptile for his environment. He's green like the leaves, has toes used for climbing trees and running away from predators.
Because he's red and he has sharp toes. Not only can he not climb trees very well but he sticks out to predators.
He can climb trees better than the last pointy-toed fella but he still sticks out because of his red skin.
This one has me confused. I know his green skin is good for fertility but the pointy toes not being rounded confuses me as to why the fertility is so high for him.
He got a 9 because he's green to blend in with his environment and he has the round toes to climb trees to escape predators.

I'm not sure why this guy got a 7 and the identical guy before him got a 9? Maybe this guys skin is a little lighter green than the last guy? Anyways his green color and round grabby toes got him a 7.
Yeah this guy is red so he sticks out to predators to be an easy snack.
This guy sticks out to predators because of his skin and he also can't easily climb trees because his toes are pointy not rounded. So he would have to stay and fight the predator and most likely die.
Green skin let's him blend into his environment but the pointy toes seem like a disadvantage to me. He can't climb trees as easily as his rounded-toe counterpart.

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they were GREEN or RED. Did being GREEN or RED make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did color matter to the fertility of reptiles?
